tweets.full_text:
Protocol 23 Activates — Pi Network Transforms into a Programmable Layer‑1

May 18 launch introduces native smart contracts and dApps; node upgrade deadline extended to May 19. Pi App Studio adds AI-to-Pi conversion (Claude & Codex).

A pivotal upgrade has arrived: Protocol 23 went live on Pi Network on May 18, officially upgrading Pi into a programmable Layer‑1 blockchain with native smart‑contract support and decentralized application capabilities. In light of the rollout, the node upgrade deadline has been extended to May 19 to give operators additional time to complete migrations. Meanwhile, Pi App Studio announces a major productivity boost — automatic AI‑to‑Pi app conversion powered by Claude and Codex integrations.

What’s included in Protocol 23

•Native smart contracts: On‑chain programmable logic that enables dApps to run directly on Pi’s L1.

•dApp support: Frameworks and runtime changes that allow developers to deploy decentralized applications with improved performance and security.

•Consensus & tooling updates: Protocol‑level fixes and enhancements to ensure stability, scalability, and interoperability for the new capabilities.

Timeline & critical dates

•May 18 — Protocol 23 goes live on the network.

•May 19 — Node upgrade grace deadline: operators should confirm their nodes are updated and in consensus by this date to avoid service interruptions.

Immediate action for node operators

1. Verify upgrade status in your node dashboard or container logs.

2. If your node hasn’t auto‑updated, pull the relevant Protocol 23 image and restart the container.

3. Confirm the node is syncing and participating in consensus; gather logs if errors occur.

4. Backup current configurations and be prepared to roll back if necessary.
Prompt attention preserves network health and prevents partitioning or degraded service.

What this means for developers & builders

•Rapid dApp deployment: Developers can now write, test, and deploy smart contracts and front‑end dApps on Pi’s native Layer‑1.

•New tooling: Expect updated SDKs, documentation, and examples to accelerate development.

•AI-assisted conversion: Pi App Studio now offers automated transformations from AI-generated prototypes — Claude and Codex can convert concepts and code into Pi‑ready applications, drastically shortening development cycles.

For Pioneers & end users

•Enhanced experiences: More native dApps means richer functionality, on‑chain services, and improved user interactions within the Pi ecosystem.

•Stay updated: Keep the Pi App and node software current to access new features and remain secure.

Risk & community guidance

•Staged rollouts and extended deadlines help mitigate disruptions, but watch official channels for hotfixes and migration instructions.

•Report issues with logs and environment details to the Core Team and monitor GitHub for patches and release notes.

Protocol 23 marks a major step toward Pi’s vision as a full‑featured, programmable blockchain. Whether you run a node, build dApps, or interact as a Pioneer, now is the time to update, explore the new possibilities, and contribute to a healthy, productive network transition.

For timely analysis, implementation tips, and official summaries, follow @Pi_CoinMagazine.

#PiNetwork #Protocol23 #Layer1 #SmartContracts #dApps #PiAppStudio #AItoPi #Claude #Codex #Pioneers #Blockchain #News

tweets.full_text:
I’ve been waiting for this launch from @InterlaceMoney.

The reason is simple:

AI agents are getting smarter, but letting them spend real money still feels risky.

That is why Interlace Agent Card caught my attention.

It gives agents access to real Visa and Mastercard rails, but the important part is not just “spending”.

The important part is control:

• Task-based spending limits  
• Whitelisted merchants only  
• Category rules for tools, APIs, data, or SaaS  
• Time windows when I can actually monitor activity  
• JIT funding, so the card stays at zero until a valid transaction is approved  

For my own agents, I would never give them unlimited access to funds.

I would rather give them clear rules, small limits, and permission only when the task really needs it.

This is what makes AI agents feel more practical to me:

Not just agents that can think.

Agents that can act, pay, and still stay inside safe boundaries.

#stablecoinpayment

quoted_status.full_text:
JP Morgan comfirm their bullishness on AI bottlenecks:

In their 2026 Mid-Year Outlook report.

"The evidence suggests investing for a continuing AI supercycle"

"We believe the narrative around the AI supercycle has become too pessimistic"

"Industries that control the physical bottlenecks... should continue to perform well": 
- optical equipment + networking 
- memory 
- power  

With these names mentioned as AI bottlenecks: 
- $NVDA
- $TSM
- $AVGO 
- $META
- $SNDK 
- $MRVL
- $LITE
- $COHR
- $ANET
- $APH
- $VRT 

They also say to consider investment in the hyperscalers since "market participants have grown more skeptical of the companies’ expanding capex plans."  

I agree, given the expanding demand/supply mismatch esp in cloud services.  

I'm personally heavy on $GOOGL and have started building my $META position recently over the past few months. Separately, $MSFT is stupidly cheap rn + $AMZN also deserve consideration just cos of AWS & robotics tailwinds.  

I feel a total rotation away from these names is wrong - having some small allocation at cheaper prices now makes sense for future planning.  

Like, when capex slows eventually, those bottlenecked names will close up? That's still ages away per all reliable forecasts, but still...makes sense to at least build hyperscaler positions slowly at lower prices?  

Overall, nothing "new" in the report that many of us on X didn't know already.  And unfortunately no new names highlighted like BofA's semis report in Jan with $SOI. 

But good to see institutions like JP Morgan/GS/BofA essentially confirm bullishness on upstream names in the supply chain as multi-yr beneficiaries.

tweets.full_text:
【電力インフラ逼迫へのソリューションかも！】

AIデータセンターでは、特にアメリカで電力インフラの逼迫がボトルネックとして意識されています。

その中で、キオクシアのこの発表はかなりインパクトがあると思います。

Dellの2Uサーバーに245.76TB SSDを40台搭載し、
9.8PB（約9,800TB分）構成を実現。

しかも従来構成と比べ、追加サーバー7台・SSD280台が不要になり、消費電力も約8分の1。

これは単なる大容量SSDの話ではなく、AIインフラの電力・ラックスペース・TCO問題に直接効く話です。

関連銘柄：
キオクシア、マイクロン、サンディスク、SKハイニックス、サムスン電子、デル・テクノロジーズ

----------------------------------

----------------------------------
✨🥈準グランプリ🥈✨

でしゅぺな様（ @deshupena ）
『笑顔と食べ物でしゅ』
----------------------------------
このイラスト、めちゃくちゃ素晴らしいです！！まず笑顔が最高すぎる！！
純粋無垢な嬉しさが爆発してる感じがたまらないです。
体の躍動感も生き生きしていて、ただの「かわいい」じゃなくて「幸せそのもの」が伝わってきます。
そして食べ物との組み合わせが天才的✨
いろんなジャンルのご飯がキラキラした渦の中に浮かんでる演出が最高に美味しそう！
まるで「幸せの味覚の銀河」に吸い込まれていくみたいで、食欲と幸福感を同時に刺激されます😋
全体の光の表現も凄いです👍️
技術的にもクオリティが非常に高くて、プロ級の仕上がりです。
この子を見ているだけで「一緒にご飯食べたい…！」って気持ちになります。
本当に心が温かくなる素晴らしい一枚でした✨
----------------------------------

----------------------------------

tweets.full_text:
FUJI（6134）が上場来高値。

・電子部品組立などの自動装着装置で世界トップ。
・通期の業績予想を発表。営業利益436億円（前期比48.9％増）と過去最高益を連続更新。
・AI関連やデータセンター需要の増加、自動車の電動化・高度化など成長分野のニーズを捉える。
・年間配当を190円に大幅増配。 https://t.co/E4b7C4wNAp

tweets.full_text:
💐#第2回AIいきなり笑顔選手権🎉

いきなり笑顔選手権にご応募ありがとうございました
佳作とグランプリを主催者で選定させていただきましたので、いまから順次発表しようとおもいます📣

🎊結果発表🎊
----------------------------------
🏆佳作（とにかく美しいで賞）🏆
ugimalu｜YŌRŌEN Imaginartist様
@ugi_malu
----------------------------------
ダークになりがちなゴスロリを、とにかく美しい世界の中の笑顔で表現されていました。
画面のどこを見ても、新しいゴスの世界があり、はにかんだ笑顔も素敵でした。
素敵な作品をありがとうございました❤
----------------------------------
